Patch Set 3:

I doubt this is right.
I think this this seqno is being sent between nodes in the pingbuf.  With your change I don't think it is being sent between nodes any more.

But I don't really know what is happening so I don't feel quite ready to give it a -1

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Looking at it again, you're 100% correct. The seqno gets added to the ping reply by default (in api-ni.c). Then LNet discovery uses it (in peer.c) when it pings different hosts. It's strange that this didn't fail a test. I'm imagine there's some LNet test case for this that doesn't run by default.

This patch was fixing some other errors. I'll undo the macro change and fix just those instead. Probably look into why no test failed.

commit.subject: LU-17000 lnet: stop abusing lo interface status
commit.message:
LU-17000 lnet: stop abusing lo interface status

The lo interface status was being abused to
transmit a sequence number. This was being
hidden via a macro. Add a proper struct field
for seqno and remove the macro. Remove another
unused macro at the same time.

Incidently, this should resolve some static
analysis issues.

NI status should be under lock, but this wasn't
a real NI status. Hence the false positive.

Addresses-Coverity-ID: 397680 ("Data race condition")

Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: I43dd6a7894d44c1533c1d00021dad6f126af18cd
